title: rubus malipoensis
intro: Rubus malipoensis, also known as the Malipo raspberry or Malipo bramble, is a species of raspberry native to the mountainous regions of southern Yunnan and northern Vietnam. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as their ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever. The health benefits of Rubus malipoensis include its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease, improve immune function, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Rubus malipoensis is traditionally used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
